beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: Past midnight. A knock — not at the door but on the wall beside it, low. The imp from before is in the doorway. Not the disguise. What he actually is.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: He holds out one hand. In it, an Asmodean Crystal. In his other hand, nothing — but he is looking at the dagger on the wall.

topics.prompt: Trade the dagger for the crystal

opening_beats.speaker: ENV
opening_beats.text: The imp waits. He knows what he wants. He knows you know what you want. The only question is whether what Hypatia left you is worth trading away.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: He takes it without ceremony. Places the crystal on the workbench. Nods once. Gone.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: He looks at the bottle. Considers it. The crystal goes back in his coat. He takes the absinthe, shrugs with a kind of philosophical resignation, and leaves.

beats.speaker: ENV
beats.text: The crystal hits the floor and shatters. He is gone before the pieces stop moving. The dust is everywhere and it is not like the dream's dust — this is just glass. Ruined.

opening_beats.speaker: 0006_WTCH
opening_beats.text: You have both. The dreamed crystal's dust and the traded one. I wondered if you would put them together. You should — they are not the same material but they bind. The dust from the dream is a sensitiser. The crystal is the active agent. Together they make Infernal Salve.

beats.speaker: 0006_WTCH
beats.text: It does not open a door. There is no door. It makes you legible to the space you are entering — the rings of Sulphera have their own logic and they need to read something in you before they let you in. The Salve is a translation.
